text:
{
  "title": "Redshift-Adaptive Pairwise Color Residual Geometry",
  "group_name": "pairwise_color_lattice_residuals",
  "family": "pairwise_color_geometry",
  "summary": "This feature group characterizes every object by robust, redshift-local deviations of its full ugriz pairwise color lattice from a reference manifold and by the principal orthogonal directions of those deviations, exposing class-distinguishing structure that is not visible in only adjacent-color projections.",
  "depends_on": [],
  "strategy": "Build the 5-vector of magnitudes m=[u,g,r,i,z] and compute the 10 pairwise color components in fixed order: u-g, u-r, u-i, u-z, g-r, g-i, g-z, r-i, r-z, i-z. On training data only, define deterministic redshift bins from quantiles with nq=32 cut points over redshift and then post-process bins so each final bin has at least m_min=max(1500, ceil(0.001*|train|)) samples by repeatedly absorbing neighboring sparse bins into the nearest non-empty side; record final boundaries and keep them frozen after fitting. For each final bin, compute component-wise median μ_b and MAD σ_b on the 10-color vectors, replace any σ_b<1e-6 by 1e-6, and center vectors x_b = x-μ_b. Compute covariance C_b of x_b, apply shrinkage C_b' = (1-ρ)C_b + ρ*diag(C_b) with ρ=0.05, then add ridge λ=1e-6*(trace(C_b')/10) to the diagonal and invert for Mahalanobis math. For each row, assign the redshift bin by clipped boundary lookup; if its bin is missing, singular, or ill-conditioned (cond(C_b')>1e8), use the nearest populated bin by redshift-midpoint distance, else fallback to global statistics at final fallback. Emit fixed numeric outputs: 10 clipped standardized residuals r=(x-μ_b)/σ_b, Mahalanobis distance d=sqrt((x-μ_b)^T (C_b'+)^-1 (x-μ_b)), and the first 3 projections of the whitened vector w=C_b'^(-1/2)(x-μ_b) onto the top-3 eigenvectors of that covariance (computed from training data in each bin, with eigenvectors sorted by descending eigenvalue and sign made deterministic by forcing the largest-magnitude loading in each axis positive). Use exactly the same binning and per-bin parameters learned from each training fold when validating, and from full train when generating test-time features.",
  "expected_signal": "The pairwise lattice captures non-local color contrasts that encode spectral-shape differences between class families, while redshift conditioning aligns the local manifold by epoch and prevents a single global center from blurring class-specific trends; robust residualization and orthogonal principal directions retain physically meaningful deviations and outlier directions where overlapping classes become separable in balanced accuracy.",
  "risk": "The 10-color residual stack is strongly redundant with raw colors, so gains can be offset by multicollinearity and model overfitting if too many correlated components are kept; sparse or noisy high-redshift regions can destabilize bin statistics even after regularization; fixed quantile boundaries may induce discontinuities for objects near bin edges unless fallback logic is carefully controlled."
}
